TABLE: system.billing.account_prices
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Contains time-bounded list prices for Databricks SKUs at the account level, including cloud, currency, and usage unit. Used to price metered usage by applying the effective rate for the usage timestamp.
Grain: One row per account_id, sku_name, cloud, currency_code, usage_unit, and price effective interval (price_start_time to price_end_time).

TABLE: system.billing.account_prices
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Apply the correct unit price to usage records based on usage time and SKU
- Audit historical price changes and effective-date pricing timelines
- Validate billing totals by recomputing charges from usage and price tables
- Support financial reporting by normalizing costs to the billed currency and unit

TABLE: system.billing.account_prices
DOC_TYPE: table_columns
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
 - price_start_time (timestamp)
 - price_end_time (timestamp)
 - sku_name (string)
 - cloud (string)
 - currency_code (string)
 - usage_unit (string)
 - pricing (struct<default:decimal(38,18)>)

TABLE: system.billing.account_prices
DOC_TYPE: table_column_details
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Cardinality: medium

 - currency_code (string)

 - usage_unit (string)
   Cardinality: medium

 - pricing (struct<default:decimal(38,18)>)
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

TABLE: system.billing.list_prices
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Historical reference table of Databricks SKU list prices by cloud and currency; a new row is emitted whenever a SKU price changes. It is used to price system.billing.usage records for accurate cost reporting over time.
Grain: One row per (account_id, sku_name, cloud, currency_code, usage_unit) price interval, effective from price_start_time until price_end_time.

TABLE: system.billing.list_prices
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Join to system.billing.usage to compute costs using the price effective at the usage time
- Analyze historical SKU price changes over time by cloud/currency
- Build forecasting/what-if models using default vs promotional vs effective list pricing
- Validate invoices or internal chargeback rates against published list pricing

TABLE: system.billing.list_prices
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.billing.usage
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name, system.billing.list_prices.date = system.billing.usage.usage_time AND system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name, system.billing.list_prices.billing_origin_product = system.billing.list_prices.billing_origin_product

 - pricing (struct<default:decimal(38,18),promotional:struct<default:decimal(38,18)>,effective_list:struct<default:decimal(38,18)>>)

TABLE: system.billing.list_prices
DOC_TYPE: column
Columns:

 - price_start_time (timestamp)

 - sku_name (string)
   Comment: Name of the SKU
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %JOBS_COMPUTE%, %SERVERLESS_REAL_TIME_INFERENCE%, 1, ALL_PURPOSE_COMPUTE, ENTERPRISE_ANTHROPIC_MODEL_SERVING
 - cloud (string)
   Comment: Name of the Cloud this price is applicable to. Possible values are AWS, AZURE, and GCP.
   Definition: Cloud provider the price applies to (AWS, AZURE, or GCP).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: AWS
 - currency_code (string)
   Comment: The currency this price is expressed in
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
   Example Filters: NULL, USD
 - usage_unit (string)
   Comment: The unit of measurement that is monetized.
   Definition: Billable unit of measure that the price is quoted in (the unit used to monetize usage).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %token%, TOKEN
 - pricing (struct<default:decimal(38,18),promotional:struct<default:decimal(38,18)>,effective_list:struct<default:decimal(38,18)>>)
   Comment: A structured data field that includes pricing info at the published list price rate. The key default will always return a single price that can be used for simple long-term estimates. The key promotional represents a temporary promotional price that all customers get which could be used for cost estimation during the temporary period. The key effective_list resolves list and promotional price, and contains the effective list price used for calculating the cost.
   Definition: Nested price details at list rate: default is the standard list price for long-term estimates; promotional.default is a temporary promotional list price; effective_list.default is the resolved list price actually used for cost calculation during the interval.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

TABLE: system.billing.usage
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Centralized, account-wide billable usage records across all Databricks workspaces and regions, used to attribute consumption to products, compute resources, and identities for cost and chargeback reporting. Includes correction mechanics (retractions/restatements) and rich metadata to join to pricing and platform objects.
Grain: One billable usage line item for an account/workspace over a specific usage time window (usage_start_time to usage_end_time), uniquely identified by record_id; corrections may appear as additional records via record_type.

TABLE: system.billing.usage
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Compute daily/monthly spend and DBU consumption by workspace, SKU, and product (with pricing joins to list_prices/account_prices).
- Allocate/charge back costs to clusters, warehouses, jobs, pipelines, endpoints, or Unity Catalog objects using usage_metadata.
- Detect cost anomalies and explain drivers by usage_type, feature flags (serverless/photon), and identity attribution.
- Build billing correction-aware reports by netting ORIGINAL vs RETRACTION/RESTATEMENT records.

TABLE: system.billing.usage
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.billing.list_prices
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.billing.list_prices.cloud = system.billing.usage.cloud AND system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name, system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name, system.billing.list_prices.cloud = system.billing.usage.cloud AND system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name AND system.billing.list_prices.usage_unit = system.billing.usage.usage_unit, system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name AND system.billing.list_prices.usage_unit = system.billing.usage.usage_unit, system.billing.list_prices.account_id = system.billing.usage.account_id AND system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name, system.billing.usage.account_id AND system.billing.list_prices.cloud = system.billing.usage.cloud AND system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name, system.billing.list_prices.account_id = system.billing.usage.account_id, system.billing.list_prices.price_date = system.billing.usage.usage_time AND system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name, system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.list_prices.sku_name, system.billing.list_prices.cloud = system.billing.usage.cloud, system.billing.list_prices.billing_origin_product = system.billing.usage.billing_origin_product AND system.billing.list_prices.usage_time = system.billing.usage.usage_time, system.billing.list_prices.billing_origin_product = system.billing.list_prices.billing_origin_product, system.billing.list_prices.account_id = system.billing.usage.account_id AND system.billing.list_prices.price_start_time = system.billing.usage.usage_time AND system.billing.list_prices.sku_name = system.billing.usage.sku_name
 - system.billing.account_prices

TABLE: system.compute.clusters
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Slow-changing dimension capturing the full historical configuration of Databricks compute (all-purpose and jobs) across workspaces and accounts. Use it to understand how cluster settings changed over time and to enrich operational, audit, and billing datasets with cluster metadata.
Grain: One row per cluster configuration version (SCD record) for a given cluster_id within a workspace/account, effective at change_time (with deletion indicated by delete_time when applicable).

TABLE: system.compute.clusters
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Enrich billing usage with cluster configuration (node types, DBR version, autoscaling settings) at the time of usage
- Track cluster ownership, naming, and policy/security mode changes over time for governance and audit investigations
- Analyze compute fleet standardization (node types, instance pools, tags) and drift from policies
- Join to node_timeline to relate configuration versions to underlying node lifecycle events

TABLE: system.compute.clusters
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.compute.node_timeline
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.compute.clusters.cluster_id = system.compute.node_timeline.cluster_id
   Extended Conditions: system.compute.clusters.workspace_id = system.compute.node_timeline.workspace_id
 - system.billing.usage
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.billing.usage.workspace_id = system.compute.clusters.workspace_id, system.billing.usage.cluster_id = system.compute.clusters.cluster_id
 - system.access.audit
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.access.audit.cluster_id = system.compute.clusters.cluster_id, system.access.audit.request_params = system.compute.clusters.cluster_id, system.access.audit.user_email = system.compute.clusters.owned_by, system.access.audit.cluster_id = system.compute.clusters.cluster_id AND system.access.audit.workspace_id = system.compute.clusters.workspace_id
 - system.compute.node_types
   Join Types: LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.compute.clusters.driver_node_type = system.compute.node_types.node_type, system.compute.clusters.worker_node_type = system.compute.node_types.node_type
 - system.access.workspaces_latest
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.workspaces_latest.workspace_id = system.compute.clusters.workspace_id

TABLE: system.compute.node_timeline
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Minute-by-minute node-level resource utilization telemetry for Databricks compute instances. It supports operational monitoring, cost/usage attribution, and troubleshooting performance issues at the instance (driver/worker) level.
Grain: One row per account_id + workspace_id + cluster_id + instance_id + minute time window (start_time to end_time).

TABLE: system.compute.node_timeline
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Identify CPU, memory, disk, or network bottlenecks on specific clusters and nodes over time
- Attribute resource saturation patterns to workloads and correlate with billing usage by cluster/workspace
- Detect unhealthy nodes (e.g., high I/O wait, heavy swap, low free disk) and trigger remediation
- Compare driver vs worker utilization to diagnose skew or driver pressure

TABLE: system.compute.node_timeline
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.compute.clusters
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.compute.clusters.cluster_id = system.compute.node_timeline.cluster_id
   Extended Conditions: system.compute.clusters.workspace_id = system.compute.node_timeline.workspace_id
 - system.access.audit
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.audit.workspace_id = system.compute.node_timeline.workspace_id
 - system.billing.usage
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.billing.usage.cluster_id = system.compute.node_timeline.cluster_id

TABLE: system.compute.node_timeline
DOC_TYPE: table_columns
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
 - workspace_id (string)
 - cluster_id (string)
 - instance_id (string)
 - start_time (timestamp)
 - end_time (timestamp)
 - driver (boolean)
 - cpu_user_percent (double)
 - cpu_system_percent (double)
 - cpu_wait_percent (double)
 - mem_used_percent (double)
 - mem_swap_percent (double)
 - network_sent_bytes (bigint)
 - network_received_bytes (bigint)
 - disk_free_bytes_per_mount_point (map<string,bigint>)
 - node_type (string)
 - private_ip (string)

   Cardinality: high

   Example Filters: 2025-11-23, 2025-12-30 00:00:00, 2025-12-30 23:59:59, 7
 - end_time (timestamp)
   Comment: End time for the record in UTC.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
   Example Filters: NULL
 - driver (boolean)
   Comment: Whether the instance is a driver or worker node.
   Definition: Indicates whether the node is the cluster driver (true) or a worker (false).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: NULL, TRUE
 - cpu_user_percent (double)
   Comment: Percentage of time the CPU spent in userland.
   Definition: CPU utilization percentage spent executing user-space (non-kernel) code during the time window.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
   Example Filters: 5, 60, 80, NULL
 - cpu_system_percent (double)
   Comment: Percentage of time the CPU spent in the kernel.
   Definition: CPU utilization percentage spent in kernel/system operations during the time window.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: NULL
 - cpu_wait_percent (double)
   Comment: Percentage of time the CPU spent waiting for I/O.
   Definition: CPU time percentage spent waiting on I/O (e.g., disk or network) during the time window.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
   Example Filters: 5, 80, NULL
 - mem_used_percent (double)
   Comment: Percentage of the compute’s memory that was used during the time period (including memory used by background processes running on the compute).
   Definition: Percentage of total node memory used during the time window, including background/system processes.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
   Example Filters: 50, 60, NULL
 - mem_swap_percent (double)
   Comment: Percentage of memory usage attributed to memory swap.
   Definition: Percentage of memory usage attributable to swapping, indicating memory pressure.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - network_sent_bytes (bigint)
   Comment: The number of bytes sent out in network traffic.
   Definition: Total bytes transmitted from the node over the network during the time window.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - network_received_bytes (bigint)
   Comment: The number of received bytes from network traffic.
   Definition: Total bytes received by the node over the network during the time window.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - disk_free_bytes_per_mount_point (map<string,bigint>)
   Comment: The disk utilization grouped by mount point. This is ephemeral storage provisioned only while the compute is running.
   Definition: Map of mount point to free bytes for the node’s ephemeral disks during the time window.
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
   Example Filters: NULL

TABLE: system.compute.node_types
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Lists the node (instance) types currently available in a Databricks account along with basic hardware specifications. Useful for understanding and selecting compute options for clusters and SQL warehouses.
Grain: One row per (account_id, node_type) representing a distinct available node type and its hardware profile.

TABLE: system.compute.node_types
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Populate UI or catalog of available node types for provisioning compute
- Validate cluster/warehouse configurations against available node types
- Compare hardware characteristics (CPU, memory, GPU) across node types
- Filter node types for workload requirements (e.g., GPU-enabled, minimum memory)

TABLE: system.compute.warehouse_events
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Captures lifecycle and scaling events emitted by Databricks SQL Warehouses, enabling operational monitoring and correlation with audit and query activity. Used to understand when a warehouse started/stopped or changed size and what capacity was running at those times.
Grain: One row per SQL warehouse event (identified by warehouse_id + event_time + event_type) within an account and workspace.

TABLE: system.compute.warehouse_events
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Captures lifecycle and scaling events emitted by Databricks SQL Warehouses, enabling operational monitoring and correlation with audit and query activity. Used to understand when a warehouse started/stopped or changed size and what capacity was running at those times.
Grain: One row per SQL warehouse event (identified by warehouse_id + event_time + event_type) within an account and workspace.

TABLE: system.compute.warehouse_events
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Build timelines of warehouse start/stop and scaling activity for operational monitoring
- Correlate warehouse events with query history to explain performance or availability changes
- Analyze autoscaling behavior (scale up/down frequency) and active cluster counts over time
- Join to warehouse metadata to report events by warehouse name, size, or configuration

TABLE: system.compute.warehouse_events
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Build timelines of warehouse start/stop and scaling activity for operational monitoring
- Correlate warehouse events with query history to explain performance or availability changes
- Analyze autoscaling behavior (scale up/down frequency) and active cluster counts over time
- Join to warehouse metadata to report events by warehouse name, size, or configuration

TABLE: system.compute.warehouse_events
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.compute.warehouses
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id = system.compute.warehouses.warehouse_id
   Extended Conditions: system.compute.warehouse_events.workspace_id = system.compute.warehouses.workspace_id
 - system.access.audit
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.audit.request_params = system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id
 - system.query.history
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.compute.warehouse_events.day = system.query.history.day AND system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id = system.query.history.warehouse_id

TABLE: system.compute.warehouses
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Stores the full configuration history for Databricks SQL warehouses, capturing each change to a warehouse’s definition over time. Useful for governance, auditing, and correlating warehouse settings with operational events and query workloads.
Grain: One row per warehouse configuration version at a point in time (identified by warehouse_id and change_time) within a workspace/account.

TABLE: system.compute.warehouses
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Audit configuration changes (size, auto-stop, min/max clusters, type/channel) over time for a warehouse
- Reconstruct point-in-time warehouse settings when investigating performance or cost anomalies
- Join to warehouse_events and query history to correlate settings with incidents and workload behavior
- Inventory and governance reporting across workspaces/accounts, including deleted warehouses

TABLE: system.compute.warehouses
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.compute.warehouse_events
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id = system.compute.warehouses.warehouse_id, system.compute.warehouse_events.event_period_key = ts.period_key AND system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id = system.compute.warehouses.warehouse_id, system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id = system.compute.warehouses.warehouse_id AND system.compute.warehouse_events.workspace_id = system.compute.warehouses.workspace_id, system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id = system.compute.warehouse_events.warehouse_id
 - system.access.workspaces_latest
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.access.workspaces_latest.workspace_id = system.compute.warehouses.workspace_id
 - system.query.history
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.compute.warehouses.workspace_id = system.query.history.workspace_id

   Comment: The ID of the SQL warehouse.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1, 862f1d757f0424f7
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace where the warehouse is deployed.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1, 1444828305810485, 3593553632678317
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the Databricks account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1
 - warehouse_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the SQL warehouse.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %shared unity%, Shared Serverless, visa_ds_65_sl_large_itr3, visa_ds_65_sl_xl_itr03, visa_wh_20_conc serverless 2X-Small
 - warehouse_type (string)
   Comment: The type of SQL warehouse. Possible values are CLASSIC, PRO, and SERVERLESS.
   Definition: SKU/class of the SQL warehouse (e.g., Classic, Pro, Serverless) that influences capabilities and billing/operation model.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - warehouse_channel (string)
   Comment: The channel of the SQL warehouse. Possible values are CURRENT and PREVIEW.
   Definition: Release channel for the warehouse runtime/feature set (e.g., Current vs Preview).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - warehouse_size (string)
   Comment: The cluster size of the SQL warehouse. Possible values are 2X_SMALL, X_SMALL, SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE, X_LARGE, 2X_LARGE, 3X_LARGE, 4X_LARGE, and 5X_LARGE.
   Definition: Configured compute size tier for the warehouse, used as the baseline capacity for serving SQL workloads.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 2X_LARGE, 3X_LARGE, 4X_LARGE, LARGE, X_LARGE
 - min_clusters (int)
   Comment: The minimum number of clusters permitted.
   Definition: Lower bound on the number of clusters the warehouse can run (minimum scaling floor).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MIN
 - max_clusters (int)
   Comment: The maximum number of clusters permitted.
   Definition: Upper bound on the number of clusters the warehouse can scale out to (maximum scaling ceiling).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - auto_stop_minutes (int)
   Comment: The number of minutes before the SQL warehouse auto-stops due to inactivity.
   Definition: Idle time threshold (in minutes) after which the warehouse is automatically stopped to reduce cost.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 10
 - tags (map<string,string>)
   Comment: Tags for the SQL warehouse.
   Definition: Key/value metadata attached to the warehouse for organization, chargeback, and policy enforcement.
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - change_time (timestamp)
   Comment: Timestamp of change to the SQL warehouse definition.
   Definition: Timestamp when this configuration version became effective (i.e., when the warehouse definition was changed).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MIN

TABLE: system.query.history
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Account-wide log of SQL statement executions on Databricks SQL warehouses, across all workspaces in the same region. Used for operational monitoring, performance troubleshooting, governance/audit, and attribution of usage to users, jobs, and other Databricks entities.
Grain: One row per SQL statement execution on a SQL warehouse, uniquely identified by statement_id (scoped within the account and associated workspace).

TABLE: system.query.history
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Investigate failed or slow statements using status, error_message, and duration breakdowns
- Attribute query activity to users, service principals, workspaces, warehouses, and triggering sources (jobs/dashboards/notebooks/alerts)
- Analyze workload characteristics (bytes/rows read & written, shuffle, spill, cache usage) for performance optimization
- Join to billing usage and lineage tables to connect cost and data access to specific statements

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: konomi.omae@databricks.com, narasimha.kamathardi@databricks.com, roy.bang@databricks.com
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: The Spark session ID.
   Definition: Spark/SQL session identifier associated with the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac5-bada-1f1a-822b-b76c37dd495e, 01f0ab91-6d43-1b75-9284-cc6360885934, 01f0c2b3-91bb-114b-ba70-c4c850b59a38
 - execution_status (string)
   Comment: The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a
JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%
 - statement_type (string)
   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).
   Definition: Wall-clock time for the statement execution in milliseconds, excluding result fetch.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600
 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
   Definition: Sum of task runtimes across all tasks/cores for the statement, reflecting total compute effort rather than wall-clock time.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a
JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%
 - statement_type (string)
   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).
   Definition: Wall-clock time for the statement execution in milliseconds, excluding result fetch.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600
 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
   Definition: Sum of task runtimes across all tasks/cores for the statement, reflecting total compute effort rather than wall-clock time.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - result_fetch_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent fetching results after execution finished.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.

   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM

   Comment: Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: The time when Databricks received the request. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.

   Cardinality: high

DOC_TYPE: column
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: ID of the account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1444828305810485, 3593553632678317
 - statement_id (string)
   Comment: The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
   Definition: Unique identifier for a specific statement execution (used to locate the run in Query History UI and to join to other telemetry).
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac6-4be9-122d-b84d-d294f99bc43f, 01f0ab91-6d47-1d9d-8a28-0ee3eabf0e7c, 01f0b985-699d-10cd-b18d-97d616f9ab48, 01f0be6e-47df-197c-bca4-3b42c1eaa2b9, 01f0bfd9-ec95-12f7-be31-52acf1ab9c58
 - executed_by (string)
   Comment: The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identity (email/username) that submitted the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: konomi.omae@databricks.com, narasimha.kamathardi@databricks.com, roy.bang@databricks.com
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: The Spark session ID.
   Definition: Spark/SQL session identifier associated with the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac5-bada-1f1a-822b-b76c37dd495e, 01f0ab91-6d43-1b75-9284-cc6360885934, 01f0c2b3-91bb-114b-ba70-c4c850b59a38
 - execution_status (string)
   Comment: The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a
JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%
 - statement_type (string)
   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).
   Definition: Wall-clock time for the statement execution in milliseconds, excluding result fetch.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600
 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
   Definition: Sum of task runtimes across all tasks/cores for the statement, reflecting total compute effort rather than wall-clock time.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - result_fetch_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent fetching results after execution finished.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - start_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time when Databricks received the request. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when Databricks received the statement request.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
   Example Filters: 2025-11-01, 2025-11-20, 2025-12-01, 2026-01-01, 5
 - end_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement execution ended, excluding result fetch time. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when statement execution completed (excluding result fetch).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
 - update_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp of the last progress/heartbeat update recorded for the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN

   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).
   Definition: Wall-clock time for the statement execution in milliseconds, excluding result fetch.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600
 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
 - update_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp of the last progress/heartbeat update recorded for the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
 - read_partitions (bigint)
   Comment: The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
   Definition: Number of partitions read after partition pruning was applied.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: ID of the account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1444828305810485, 3593553632678317
 - statement_id (string)
   Comment: The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
   Definition: Unique identifier for a specific statement execution (used to locate the run in Query History UI and to join to other telemetry).
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac6-4be9-122d-b84d-d294f99bc43f, 01f0ab91-6d47-1d9d-8a28-0ee3eabf0e7c, 01f0b985-699d-10cd-b18d-97d616f9ab48, 01f0be6e-47df-197c-bca4-3b42c1eaa2b9, 01f0bfd9-ec95-12f7-be31-52acf1ab9c58
 - executed_by (string)
   Comment: The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identity (email/username) that submitted the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: konomi.omae@databricks.com, narasimha.kamathardi@databricks.com, roy.bang@databricks.com
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: The Spark session ID.
   Definition: Spark/SQL session identifier associated with the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac5-bada-1f1a-822b-b76c37dd495e, 01f0ab91-6d43-1b75-9284-cc6360885934, 01f0c2b3-91bb-114b-ba70-c4c850b59a38
 - execution_status (string)
   Comment: The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a
JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%
 - statement_type (string)
   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).
   Definition: Wall-clock time for the statement execution in milliseconds, excluding result fetch.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600
 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
   Definition: Sum of task runtimes across all tasks/cores for the statement, reflecting total compute effort rather than wall-clock time.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - result_fetch_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent fetching results after execution finished.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - start_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time when Databricks received the request. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when Databricks received the statement request.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
   Example Filters: 2025-11-01, 2025-11-20, 2025-12-01, 2026-01-01, 5
 - end_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement execution ended, excluding result fetch time. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when statement execution completed (excluding result fetch).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
 - update_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp of the last progress/heartbeat update recorded for the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
 - read_partitions (bigint)
   Comment: The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
   Definition: Number of partitions read after partition pruning was applied.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - pruned_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of pruned files during table partition and file pruning.
   Definition: Count of files eliminated by partition and file pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.

   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%

   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.

   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).

   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM

 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM

   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
 - read_partitions (bigint)
   Comment: The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
   Definition: Number of partitions read after partition pruning was applied.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - pruned_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of pruned files during table partition and file pruning.

   Comment: The number of files read after table partition and file pruning.

   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
   Definition: Sum of task runtimes across all tasks/cores for the statement, reflecting total compute effort rather than wall-clock time.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - result_fetch_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent fetching results after execution finished.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - start_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time when Databricks received the request. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when Databricks received the statement request.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
   Example Filters: 2025-11-01, 2025-11-20, 2025-12-01, 2026-01-01, 5
 - end_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement execution ended, excluding result fetch time. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when statement execution completed (excluding result fetch).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
 - update_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp of the last progress/heartbeat update recorded for the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
 - read_partitions (bigint)
   Comment: The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
   Definition: Number of partitions read after partition pruning was applied.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - pruned_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of pruned files during table partition and file pruning.
   Definition: Count of files eliminated by partition and file pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - read_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of files read after table partition and file pruning.
   Definition: Count of files actually read after partition/file pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - read_rows (bigint)
   Comment: The number of rows output from scans after table partition, file, in file and row pruning.
   Definition: Number of rows produced by scan operators after pruning (table/file/row pruning effects applied).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM

TABLE: system.query.history
DOC_TYPE: column
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: ID of the account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1444828305810485, 3593553632678317
 - statement_id (string)
   Comment: The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
   Definition: Unique identifier for a specific statement execution (used to locate the run in Query History UI and to join to other telemetry).
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac6-4be9-122d-b84d-d294f99bc43f, 01f0ab91-6d47-1d9d-8a28-0ee3eabf0e7c, 01f0b985-699d-10cd-b18d-97d616f9ab48, 01f0be6e-47df-197c-bca4-3b42c1eaa2b9, 01f0bfd9-ec95-12f7-be31-52acf1ab9c58
 - executed_by (string)
   Comment: The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identity (email/username) that submitted the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: konomi.omae@databricks.com, narasimha.kamathardi@databricks.com, roy.bang@databricks.com
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: The Spark session ID.
   Definition: Spark/SQL session identifier associated with the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac5-bada-1f1a-822b-b76c37dd495e, 01f0ab91-6d43-1b75-9284-cc6360885934, 01f0c2b3-91bb-114b-ba70-c4c850b59a38
 - execution_status (string)
   Comment: The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a

   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.

TABLE: system.query.history
DOC_TYPE: column
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: ID of the account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1444828305810485, 3593553632678317
 - statement_id (string)
   Comment: The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
   Definition: Unique identifier for a specific statement execution (used to locate the run in Query History UI and to join to other telemetry).
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac6-4be9-122d-b84d-d294f99bc43f, 01f0ab91-6d47-1d9d-8a28-0ee3eabf0e7c, 01f0b985-699d-10cd-b18d-97d616f9ab48, 01f0be6e-47df-197c-bca4-3b42c1eaa2b9, 01f0bfd9-ec95-12f7-be31-52acf1ab9c58
 - executed_by (string)
   Comment: The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identity (email/username) that submitted the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: konomi.omae@databricks.com, narasimha.kamathardi@databricks.com, roy.bang@databricks.com
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: The Spark session ID.
   Definition: Spark/SQL session identifier associated with the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac5-bada-1f1a-822b-b76c37dd495e, 01f0ab91-6d43-1b75-9284-cc6360885934, 01f0c2b3-91bb-114b-ba70-c4c850b59a38
 - execution_status (string)
   Comment: The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a
JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%
 - statement_type (string)
   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600

   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.

   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.

   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes.    Definition: Timestamp when statement execution completed (excluding result fetch).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
 - update_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp of the last progress/heartbeat update recorded for the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
 - read_partitions (bigint)
   Comment: The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
   Definition: Number of partitions read after partition pruning was applied.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - pruned_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of pruned files during table partition and file pruning.
   Definition: Count of files eliminated by partition and file pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - read_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of files read after table partition and file pruning.
   Definition: Count of files actually read after partition/file pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - read_rows (bigint)
   Comment: The number of rows output from scans after table partition, file, in file and row pruning.
   Definition: Number of rows produced by scan operators after pruning (table/file/row pruning effects applied).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - produced_rows (bigint)
   Comment: Total number of rows returned by the statement.
   Definition: Total number of rows returned/produced by the statement result.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - read_bytes (bigint)
   Comment: The number of bytes of files read after IO pruning.
   Definition: Bytes read from files after I/O pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, NULL

TABLE: system.query.history
DOC_TYPE: column
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: ID of the account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1444828305810485, 3593553632678317
 - statement_id (string)
   Comment: The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
   Definition: Unique identifier for a specific statement execution (used to locate the run in Query History UI and to join to other telemetry).
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac6-4be9-122d-b84d-d294f99bc43f, 01f0ab91-6d47-1d9d-8a28-0ee3eabf0e7c, 01f0b985-699d-10cd-b18d-97d616f9ab48, 01f0be6e-47df-197c-bca4-3b42c1eaa2b9, 01f0bfd9-ec95-12f7-be31-52acf1ab9c58
 - executed_by (string)
   Comment: The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identity (email/username) that submitted the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: konomi.omae@databricks.com, narasimha.kamathardi@databricks.com, roy.bang@databricks.com
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: The Spark session ID.
   Definition: Spark/SQL session identifier associated with the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac5-bada-1f1a-822b-b76c37dd495e, 01f0ab91-6d43-1b75-9284-cc6360885934, 01f0c2b3-91bb-114b-ba70-c4c850b59a38
 - execution_status (string)
   Comment: The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a
JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%
 - statement_type (string)
   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).
   Definition: Wall-clock time for the statement execution in milliseconds, excluding result fetch.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600
 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM

   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.

   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes.    Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM

   Comment: Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
