TABLE: system.query.history
DOC_TYPE: column
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: ID of the account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 1444828305810485, 3593553632678317
 - statement_id (string)
   Comment: The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
   Definition: Unique identifier for a specific statement execution (used to locate the run in Query History UI and to join to other telemetry).
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac6-4be9-122d-b84d-d294f99bc43f, 01f0ab91-6d47-1d9d-8a28-0ee3eabf0e7c, 01f0b985-699d-10cd-b18d-97d616f9ab48, 01f0be6e-47df-197c-bca4-3b42c1eaa2b9, 01f0bfd9-ec95-12f7-be31-52acf1ab9c58
 - executed_by (string)
   Comment: The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identity (email/username) that submitted the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: konomi.omae@databricks.com, narasimha.kamathardi@databricks.com, roy.bang@databricks.com
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: The Spark session ID.
   Definition: Spark/SQL session identifier associated with the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 01f0aac5-bada-1f1a-822b-b76c37dd495e, 01f0ab91-6d43-1b75-9284-cc6360885934, 01f0c2b3-91bb-114b-ba70-c4c850b59a38
 - execution_status (string)
   Comment: The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
   Definition: Terminal status of the statement execution (e.g., FINISHED, FAILED, CANCELED).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FINISHED
 - compute (struct<type:string,cluster_id:string,warehouse_id:string>)
   Comment: A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
   Definition: Compute descriptor for where the statement ran, including compute type and the warehouse/cluster identifier when applicable.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_by_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user who ran the statement.
   Definition: User identifier of the submitter of the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_text (string)
   Comment: Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
   Definition: SQL text submitted for execution (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: % join %, %FROM aircraft_stats2 a
JOIN%, %FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * 
    FROM aircraft_stats2%, %SELECT * FROM aircraft_stats2%
 - statement_type (string)
   Comment: The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
   Definition: High-level SQL operation category for the statement (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, ALTER, COPY).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: ALTER, CREATE, DELETE, DESC, DESCRIBE
 - error_message (string)
   Comment: Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
   Definition: Failure description when execution_status is FAILED (may be blank when customer-managed keys are enabled).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - client_application (string)
   Comment: Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
   Definition: Application that initiated the statement (e.g., Databricks SQL, Tableau, Power BI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %BI%, %Power%, Databricks Dbt, Databricks SQL Editor, Databricks SQL Genie Space
 - client_driver (string)
   Comment: The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
   Definition: Connector/driver used to connect and run the statement (e.g., JDBC/ODBC/SQL driver variants).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).
   Definition: Wall-clock time for the statement execution in milliseconds, excluding result fetch.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, 3600000, 600
 - waiting_for_compute_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned/started.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent queued waiting for available warehouse capacity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - execution_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent executing the statement (runtime phase).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - compilation_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent in analysis/metadata loading and optimization before execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - total_task_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
   Definition: Sum of task runtimes across all tasks/cores for the statement, reflecting total compute effort rather than wall-clock time.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - result_fetch_duration_ms (bigint)
   Comment: Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
   Definition: Time in milliseconds spent fetching results after execution finished.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - start_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time when Databricks received the request. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when Databricks received the statement request.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
   Example Filters: 2025-11-01, 2025-11-20, 2025-12-01, 2026-01-01, 5
 - end_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement execution ended, excluding result fetch time. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when statement execution completed (excluding result fetch).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
 - update_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp of the last progress/heartbeat update recorded for the statement execution.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
 - read_partitions (bigint)
   Comment: The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
   Definition: Number of partitions read after partition pruning was applied.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - pruned_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of pruned files during table partition and file pruning.
   Definition: Count of files eliminated by partition and file pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - read_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of files read after table partition and file pruning.
   Definition: Count of files actually read after partition/file pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - read_rows (bigint)
   Comment: The number of rows output from scans after table partition, file, in file and row pruning.
   Definition: Number of rows produced by scan operators after pruning (table/file/row pruning effects applied).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - produced_rows (bigint)
   Comment: Total number of rows returned by the statement.
   Definition: Total number of rows returned/produced by the statement result.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - read_bytes (bigint)
   Comment: The number of bytes of files read after IO pruning.
   Definition: Bytes read from files after I/O pruning.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0, NULL
 - read_io_cache_percent (tinyint)
   Comment: The percentage of bytes of persistent data read from the IO cache.
   Definition: Percent of persistent data bytes served from the I/O cache rather than remote storage.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - from_result_cache (boolean)
   Comment: TRUE indicates that the statement result was fetched from the cache.
   Definition: Indicates whether the statement result was served from the result cache.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: FALSE
 - spilled_local_bytes (bigint)
   Comment: Size of data, in bytes, temporarily written to disk while executing the statement.
   Definition: Bytes spilled to local disk during execution due to memory pressure.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - written_bytes (bigint)
   Comment: The size in bytes of persistent data written to cloud object storage.
   Definition: Bytes of persistent data written to cloud object storage by the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: NULL
 - shuffle_read_bytes (bigint)
   Comment: The total amount of data in bytes sent over the network.
   Definition: Bytes transferred over the network for shuffle reads (inter-node data exchange).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0
 - query_source (struct<job_info:struct<job_id:string,job_run_id:string,job_task_run_id:string>,legacy_dashboard_id:string,dashboard_id:string,alert_id:string,notebook_id:string,sql_query_id:string,genie_space_id:string,pipeline_info:struct<pipeline_id:string,update_id:string>>)
   Comment: A struct that contains key-value pairs representing one or more Databricks entities that were involved in the execution of this statement, such as jobs, notebooks, or dashboards. This field only records Databricks entities and are not sorted by execution order. Statement executions that contain multiple IDs indicate that the execution was triggered by multiple entities: for example, an Alert may trigger on a Job result and call a SQL Query, so all three IDs will be populated within query_source.
   Definition: Structured references to Databricks entities involved in triggering/executing the statement (e.g., job runs, dashboards, alerts, notebooks, SQL queries, pipelines).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_as_user_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user or service principal whose privilege was used to run the statement.
   Definition: Identity (user/service principal) whose privileges were used to execute the statement (impersonation/effective identity).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - executed_as (string)
   Comment: The name of the user or service principal whose privilege was used to run the statement.
   Definition: Name of the user or service principal whose privileges were used to execute the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: %@serviceprincipal%, %mitch.desmond%
 - written_rows (bigint)
   Comment: Total number of rows of persistent data written to cloud object storage.
   Definition: Number of persistent rows written to cloud object storage by the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - written_files (bigint)
   Comment: The number of files of persistent data written to cloud object storage.
   Definition: Number of persistent files written to cloud object storage by the statement.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - cache_origin_statement_id (string)
   Comment: Statement id of query that inserted result in cache when result is fetched from cache, otherwise store the query's statement id.
   Definition: Statement execution id that originally populated the cached result when this statement was served from cache; otherwise equals the current statement_id.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

TABLE: system.access.assistant_events
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Captures audit-style records for Databricks Assistant chat and inline interactions to measure engagement and adoption across accounts and workspaces. Useful for analyzing usage trends and user participation over time.
Grain: One row per Assistant interaction event, uniquely identified by event_id (scoped within an account/workspace).

TABLE: system.access.audit
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: System access audit logs capturing security- and compliance-relevant actions across Databricks account and workspaces. Used to monitor who did what, when, from where, and with what outcome for investigations and operational oversight.
Grain: One row per audited event (single action/request) identified by event_id (and typically correlated with request_id and event_time) within an account/workspace.

TABLE: system.access.audit
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: System access audit logs capturing security- and compliance-relevant actions across Databricks account and workspaces. Used to monitor who did what, when, from where, and with what outcome for investigations and operational oversight.
Grain: One row per audited event (single action/request) identified by event_id (and typically correlated with request_id and event_time) within an account/workspace.

TABLE: system.access.clean_room_events
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Records audit-style events for Databricks Clean Rooms across the account, capturing who did what and when, plus event-specific metadata (creation, deletion, notebook runs, asset changes, reviews). Includes regional data aggregated across the account.
Grain: One row per clean room event (uniquely identified by event_id) for a given account/metastore at a specific event_time.

TABLE: system.access.clean_room_events
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Records audit-style events for Databricks Clean Rooms across the account, capturing who did what and when, plus event-specific metadata (creation, deletion, notebook runs, asset changes, reviews). Includes regional data aggregated across the account.
Grain: One row per clean room event (uniquely identified by event_id) for a given account/metastore at a specific event_time.

TABLE: system.access.column_lineage
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: System table capturing column-level lineage for each Spark write event affecting a Unity Catalog table or path, including runs from notebooks, jobs, pipelines, and dashboards. It links source columns/objects to target columns/objects for governance, impact analysis, and auditing.
Grain: One row per lineage mapping from a single source column (or source object) to a single target column for a specific write event; natural keys include record_id (row PK) and event_id (groups rows for the same event), with optional entity_type/entity_id/entity_run_id context.

TABLE: system.access.column_lineage
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: System table capturing column-level lineage for each Spark write event affecting a Unity Catalog table or path, including runs from notebooks, jobs, pipelines, and dashboards. It links source columns/objects to target columns/objects for governance, impact analysis, and auditing.
Grain: One row per lineage mapping from a single source column (or source object) to a single target column for a specific write event; natural keys include record_id (row PK) and event_id (groups rows for the same event), with optional entity_type/entity_id/entity_run_id context.

TABLE: system.access.inbound_network
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: This table captures inbound network access-control policy evaluation events for incoming requests, useful for monitoring and auditing network access decisions. Each record reflects the outcome and context of evaluating access policies for a single inbound request.
Grain: One row per inbound network request access-control policy evaluation event.

TABLE: system.access.inbound_network
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: This table captures inbound network access-control policy evaluation events for incoming requests, useful for monitoring and auditing network access decisions. Each record reflects the outcome and context of evaluating access policies for a single inbound request.
Grain: One row per inbound network request access-control policy evaluation event.

TABLE: system.access.outbound_network
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Records outbound network access attempts that were denied from Databricks compute, capturing what destination was blocked and when it occurred. Used to audit and troubleshoot egress restrictions and blocked internet/storage access.
Grain: One row per denied outbound network access event (uniquely identified by event_id) within a Databricks account and workspace.

TABLE: system.access.outbound_network
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Records outbound network access attempts that were denied from Databricks compute, capturing what destination was blocked and when it occurred. Used to audit and troubleshoot egress restrictions and blocked internet/storage access.
Grain: One row per denied outbound network access event (uniquely identified by event_id) within a Databricks account and workspace.

TABLE: system.access.table_lineage
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Records read/write lineage events captured from Spark operations against Unity Catalog tables or direct paths, across notebooks, jobs, pipelines, and SQL assets. Used to trace data movement and dependencies and to enrich lineage with execution metadata via joins to query history, jobs, pipelines, and audit/billing tables.
Grain: One row per lineage edge captured for a single read or write event, linking a source (table/view/path) to a target (table/view/path) within a specific entity run; primary key is record_id, with event_id grouping multiple rows belonging to the same execution event.

TABLE: system.access.table_lineage
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Records read/write lineage events captured from Spark operations against Unity Catalog tables or direct paths, across notebooks, jobs, pipelines, and SQL assets. Used to trace data movement and dependencies and to enrich lineage with execution metadata via joins to query history, jobs, pipelines, and audit/billing tables.
Grain: One row per lineage edge captured for a single read or write event, linking a source (table/view/path) to a target (table/view/path) within a specific entity run; primary key is record_id, with event_id grouping multiple rows belonging to the same execution event.

TABLE: system.access.workspaces_latest
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Slow-changing dimension containing the latest known metadata for every Databricks workspace in an account, used to enrich other system tables with workspace attributes and status.
Grain: One row per workspace_id (current/latest record for that workspace) within an account_id.

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: System fact table that breaks each Databricks job run into time slices, enabling timeline-based monitoring and correlation to compute usage and billing. It supports tracking run progress, outcomes, and run metadata over the course of execution.
Grain: One row per (account_id, workspace_id, job_id, run_id, period_start_time, period_end_time) time slice for a job run.

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_task_run_timeline
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Tracks job task run execution over time by emitting time-sliced records for each ongoing task run, enabling monitoring of duration, compute usage, and outcomes. It supports operational observability and cost/performance analysis for Databricks Jobs task runs.
Grain: One row per job task run time slice, keyed by (account_id, workspace_id, job_id, run_id, task_key, period_start_time, period_end_time).

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_tasks
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Slow-changing dimension (SCD2) history of Databricks job task configuration for Lakeflow jobs, capturing every task definition change and deletion over time. Use it to audit how a job’s task graph and settings evolved within an account/workspace.
Grain: One versioned job task configuration record (SCD2 row) for a given (account_id, workspace_id, job_id, task_key) as of change_time; newer rows logically replace prior versions, and delete_time indicates user deletion.

TABLE: system.lakeflow.jobs
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Slow-changing dimension (SCD2) history of Databricks Lakeflow job configurations, capturing every version of a job record when it is created, modified, paused, or deleted. Use it to understand what a job’s configuration looked like at any point in time and to enrich job runs, audit, lineage, and billing analyses.
Grain: One row per (account_id, workspace_id, job_id) job configuration version, effective at change_time (with delete_time indicating logical deletion when present).

TABLE: system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Fact table for Lakeflow pipeline update execution timelines, capturing time-sliced intervals and metadata for ongoing or completed updates. Used to analyze update duration, triggers, outcomes, and resource context per pipeline update.
Grain: One row per workspace_id + pipeline_id + update_id + request_id + (period_start_time, period_end_time) time slice for a pipeline update.

TABLE: system.lakeflow.pipelines
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Historical (SCD2) dimension of Lakeflow pipeline configurations, capturing each version of a pipeline’s definition and metadata over time, including modifications and deletions. Useful for auditing configuration drift and attributing pipeline behavior to the configuration active at a point in time.
Grain: One row per pipeline configuration version (SCD2) for a given workspace_id and pipeline_id, effective as of change_time (and logically ended when replaced by a newer row or when delete_time is set).

TABLE: system.lakeflow.zerobus_ingest
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: System-managed table intended to store metadata and/or payload details for records ingested via Zerobus Ingest within Lakeflow. The table definition currently exposes no columns, so its usable semantics cannot be derived from the schema.
Grain: Unknown (no columns are defined to infer a per-row entity or natural key).

TABLE: system.lakeflow.zerobus_stream
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Stores event and lifecycle details for Zerobus Ingest streams, including when streams are opened/closed and any errors encountered. Used to monitor and troubleshoot streaming ingestion activity and relate it to Zerobus ingest records.
Grain: One row per Zerobus stream event record, keyed primarily by stream_id with associated event_time; may include multiple rows per stream_id over time.

TABLE: system.mlflow.experiments_latest
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Provides the latest snapshot of MLflow experiments created in Databricks-managed MLflow, including creation, update, and soft-deletion timestamps. Used to inventory experiments and track their lifecycle across accounts and workspaces.
Grain: One row per MLflow experiment (experiment_id) representing its latest known state in a given workspace/account.

TABLE: system.mlflow.run_metrics_history
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: This table stores time-series metric points logged to MLflow for model training, evaluation, or agent development runs. It enables analysis of metric trajectories over time and steps within a run.
Grain: One row per MLflow metric datapoint (run_id + metric_name + metric_time/metric_step), uniquely identified by record_id.

TABLE: system.mlflow.runs_latest
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Latest-state snapshot of Databricks-managed MLflow runs, capturing core run metadata and a rolled-up view of logged metrics. Use it to inventory and filter runs without scanning full metric histories.
Grain: One row per MLflow run (run_id) in a workspace/account, representing the most recent known state of that run including summary metrics.

TABLE: system.serving.endpoint_usage
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Per-request usage and attribution facts for Databricks Model Serving endpoints, including token/character counts and response status. Used to analyze and allocate serving consumption (especially LLM usage) by endpoint/served entity and caller context.
Grain: One row per serving invocation request received by a served entity on a serving endpoint, identifiable by databricks_request_id (and optionally client_request_id) within a workspace.

TABLE: system.serving.endpoint_usage
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Per-request usage and attribution facts for Databricks Model Serving endpoints, including token/character counts and response status. Used to analyze and allocate serving consumption (especially LLM usage) by endpoint/served entity and caller context.
Grain: One row per serving invocation request received by a served entity on a serving endpoint, identifiable by databricks_request_id (and optionally client_request_id) within a workspace.

TABLE: system.serving.served_entities
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Tracks every entity served via Databricks Model Serving and the served entity’s configuration as it changes across endpoint configuration versions. Useful for inventory, governance, and cost/usage attribution of serving endpoints and their deployed entities.
Grain: One row per served entity per endpoint configuration version (and change event), uniquely identified by served_entity_id together with endpoint_config_version (and change_time for sequencing).

TABLE: system.storage.predictive_optimization_operations_history
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Tracks the history of Predictive Optimization operations (e.g., compaction and vacuum) executed on tables, including timing, outcome, and associated usage for cost/chargeback analysis. Use it to audit optimization activity and understand operational impact across workspaces and metastores.
Grain: One row per predictive optimization operation execution on a specific table (keyed by operation_id; associated to a workspace_id and table_id).

TABLE: system.storage.predictive_optimization_operations_history
DOC_TYPE: table_summary
Purpose: Tracks the history of Predictive Optimization operations (e.g., compaction and vacuum) executed on tables, including timing, outcome, and associated usage for cost/chargeback analysis. Use it to audit optimization activity and understand operational impact across workspaces and metastores.
Grain: One row per predictive optimization operation execution on a specific table (keyed by operation_id; associated to a workspace_id and table_id).

TABLE: system.access.assistant_events
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Track Assistant adoption over time by workspace/account using event_time or event_date
- Identify top users or teams engaging with Assistant via initiated_by
- Segment usage by client/source using user_agent (e.g., UI vs other origins)
- Join to workspaces_latest to enrich events with workspace metadata for reporting

TABLE: system.access.assistant_events
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.access.workspaces_latest
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.access.assistant_events.workspace_id = system.access.workspaces_latest.workspace_id

TABLE: system.access.audit
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Security investigations and incident response (trace suspicious actions by user/session/IP)
- Compliance reporting (access to data/permissions changes by date, service, action)
- Operational monitoring (error rates and failed actions by service/action/status_code)
- Correlation with other system tables (queries, jobs, clusters, billing usage) using workspace_id/request_id/event_id

TABLE: system.access.audit
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.access.workspaces_latest
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.access.audit.workspace_id = system.access.workspaces_latest.workspace_id
 - system.query.history
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.access.audit.request_params = system.query.history.statement_text, system.access.audit.request_id = system.query.history.statement_id, system.access.audit.request_params = system.query.history.statement_id
 - system.lakeflow.jobs
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.audit.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.workspace_id
 - system.access.table_lineage
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.access.audit.request_id = system.access.table_lineage.entity_run_id AND system.access.audit.workspace_id = system.access.table_lineage.workspace_id, system.access.audit.workspace_id = system.access.table_lineage.workspace_id, system.access.audit.event_id = system.access.table_lineage.entity_run_id AND system.access.audit.workspace_id = system.access.table_lineage.workspace_id, system.access.table_lineage.table_full_name = tables_with_classifications.table_full_name

   Comment: A map of key/value pairs with the request parameters. Request parameters vary by request type. See [documentation of request parameters per action](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/admin/account-settings/audit-logs) for details.
   Definition: Key/value parameters supplied with the request (shape varies by action); often contains resource identifiers and operation arguments.
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: NULL, sql
 - response (struct<status_code:int,error_message:string,result:string>)
   Comment: Struct of response, includes status_code, error_messages, and where applicable a result string. status_code is an HTTP status code.
   Definition: Response details for the request, including HTTP status_code, any error_message, and an optional result summary.
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - audit_level (string)
   Comment: Indicates whether the audit event is at the workspace or account level. Either `ACCOUNT_LEVEL` or `WORKSPACE_LEVEL`.
   Definition: Scope of the audit event (ACCOUNT_LEVEL vs WORKSPACE_LEVEL).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: WORKSPACE_LEVEL
 - event_id (string)
   Comment: A unique identifier of the audit event.
   Definition: Unique identifier for the audit event record; serves as the natural primary key for the table.
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - identity_metadata (struct<run_by:string,run_as:string,acting_resource:string>)
   Comment: A struct with the identities from the audit event. This includes `run_by` (the identity who initiated the event) and `run_as` (the identity being used for authorisation purposes). See [Auditing group dedicated compute activity documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/compute/group-access#auditing-group-dedicated-compute-activity) for more details.
   Definition: Additional identity context such as run_by (initiator), run_as (authorization identity), and acting_resource for delegated/impersonated or group compute scenarios.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

TABLE: system.access.audit
DOC_TYPE: column
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
   Comment: The Databricks account identifier associated with the audit event.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The Databricks workspace in which the audit event took place. For account-level events, the workspace_id will be 0.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: 0, 1444828305810485
 - version (string)
   Comment: Audit log schema version
   Definition: Audit log schema version for the event record; helps interpret available fields and formats over time.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - event_time (timestamp)
   Comment: Timestamp of the event
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
   Example Filters: 0, 2025-12-04T02:40:00.000+00:00, 2025-12-04T02:41:13.327+00:00, 2025-12-04T02:51:46.557+00:00
 - event_date (date)
   Comment: The calendar date when the event or action occurred. Useful for filtering and aggregating events by day.
   Definition: Calendar date of the event, primarily for partitioning, filtering, and daily aggregations.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
   Example Filters: 2025-12%, 2025-12-03T00:00:00.000Z
 - source_ip_address (string)
   Comment: The IP address from which the request originated.
   Definition: Originating client IP address for the request that generated the audit event.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - user_agent (string)
   Comment: The user agent string or identifier describing the client, browser, or tool that initiated the request (e.g., web browser, API client, CLI)
   Definition: Client identifier string (browser/CLI/SDK/tool) used to make the request; useful for detecting automation vs interactive access.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - session_id (string)
   Comment: Unique identifier for the session in which the request was made. Sessions group related actions by a user or service over a period of time
   Definition: Identifier that groups multiple audit events belonging to the same login/session context over a period of activity.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - user_identity (struct<email:string,subject_name:string>)
   Comment: A map of key/value pairs that can be used to identify the person or service principal that initiated the request. Contains keys such as email and subject_name. An email of System-User is used for background system tasks.
   Definition: Identity attributes of the actor that initiated the event (for example email and subject_name), including system/background actors (e.g., System-User).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - service_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the Databricks service that generated the audit event. See [service documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/admin/account-settings/audit-logs#audit-log-services) for details.
   Definition: Databricks product/service emitting the event (e.g., clusters, jobs, SQL, permissions), used to categorize activity.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: NULL, accounts, aibiGenie, apps, clusters
 - action_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the action that has been performed as part of the audit event. Action names vary depending on the Databricks service (service_name). See [documentation of actions per service](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/admin/account-settings/audit-logs) for details.
   Definition: Action performed within the service (varies by service_name), used to identify the specific operation being audited.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: %GetSpace%, %Group%, %create%, %group%, accessApp
 - request_id (string)
   Comment: A unique identifier of the request.
   Definition: Unique identifier for the underlying request, used to correlate related logs and downstream system records.
   Cardinality: near-unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - request_params (map<string,string>)
   Comment: A map of key/value pairs with the request parameters. Request parameters vary by request type. See [documentation of request parameters per action](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/admin/account-settings/audit-logs) for details.
   Definition: Key/value parameters supplied with the request (shape varies by action); often contains resource identifiers and operation arguments.
   Cardinality: unknown

 - response (struct<status_code:int,error_message:string,result:string>)
   Comment: Struct of response, includes status_code, error_messages, and where applicable a result string. status_code is an HTTP status code.
   Definition: Response details for the request, including HTTP status_code, any error_message, and an optional result summary.
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - audit_level (string)
   Comment: Indicates whether the audit event is at the workspace or account level. Either `ACCOUNT_LEVEL` or `WORKSPACE_LEVEL`.
   Definition: Scope of the audit event (ACCOUNT_LEVEL vs WORKSPACE_LEVEL).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: WORKSPACE_LEVEL
 - event_id (string)
   Comment: A unique identifier of the audit event.
   Definition: Unique identifier for the audit event record; serves as the natural primary key for the table.
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - identity_metadata (struct<run_by:string,run_as:string,acting_resource:string>)
   Comment: A struct with the identities from the audit event. This includes `run_by` (the identity who initiated the event) and `run_as` (the identity being used for authorisation purposes). See [Auditing group dedicated compute activity documentation](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/compute/group-access#auditing-group-dedicated-compute-activity) for more details.

TABLE: system.access.clean_room_events
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Audit collaborator actions on clean rooms (create/delete, asset changes, notebook runs)
- Operational monitoring of clean room notebook executions and outcomes
- Compliance reporting for clean room configuration and egress/compliance settings at creation time
- Investigate asset review activity and approval signals over time

TABLE: system.access.column_lineage
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Trace which upstream columns and sources feed a given target column (impact analysis)
- Attribute data transformations to the originating job/notebook/query run for auditing
- Build end-to-end lineage graphs by joining to table_lineage and query history (statement_id)
- Monitor changes in lineage over time by event_date/event_time and created_by

TABLE: system.access.column_lineage
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Trace which upstream columns and sources feed a given target column (impact analysis)
- Attribute data transformations to the originating job/notebook/query run for auditing
- Build end-to-end lineage graphs by joining to table_lineage and query history (statement_id)
- Monitor changes in lineage over time by event_date/event_time and created_by

TABLE: system.access.column_lineage
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.access.table_lineage
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.access.column_lineage.source_table_catalog = system.access.table_lineage.source_table_catalog AND system.access.column_lineage.source_table_schema = system.access.table_lineage.source_table_schema
 - system.query.history
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.column_lineage.statement_id = system.query.history.statement_id

TABLE: system.access.column_lineage
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.access.table_lineage
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.access.column_lineage.source_table_catalog = system.access.table_lineage.source_table_catalog AND system.access.column_lineage.source_table_schema = system.access.table_lineage.source_table_schema
 - system.query.history
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.column_lineage.statement_id = system.query.history.statement_id

TABLE: system.access.column_lineage
DOC_TYPE: table_columns
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
 - metastore_id (string)
 - workspace_id (string)
 - entity_type (string)
 - entity_id (string)
 - entity_run_id (string)
 - source_table_full_name (string)
 - source_table_catalog (string)
 - source_table_schema (string)
 - source_table_name (string)
 - source_path (string)
 - source_type (string)
 - source_column_name (string)
 - target_table_full_name (string)

   Definition: Three-level Unity Catalog name (catalog.schema.table) of the target table written by the event when the target is a UC table.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - target_table_catalog (string)
   Comment: The catalog of the target table.
   Definition: Catalog containing the target table (when the target is a UC table).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: __databricks_internal_catalog%
 - target_table_schema (string)
   Comment: The schema of the target table.
   Definition: Schema containing the target table (when the target is a UC table).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - target_table_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the target table.
   Definition: Unqualified name of the target table (when the target is a UC table).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: NULL
 - target_path (string)
   Comment: Location in cloud storage of the target table.
   Definition: Cloud storage location of the target table or written path.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - target_type (string)
   Comment: The type of the target. The value is TABLE, PATH, VIEW, MATERIALIZED_VIEW, METRIC_VIEW, or STREAMING_TABLE.
   Definition: Classification of the target object (TABLE, PATH, VIEW, MATERIALIZED_VIEW, METRIC_VIEW, or STREAMING_TABLE).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - target_column_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the target column.
   Definition: Name of the target column produced/updated by the write event for this lineage record.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - created_by (string)
   Comment: The user who generated this lineage. This can be a Databricks username, a Databricks service principal ID, “System-User”, or NULL if the user information cannot be captured.
   Definition: User or service principal responsible for generating the lineage event; may be System-User or NULL if not captured.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - event_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The timestamp when the lineage was generated. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when the lineage record was generated for the write event (timezone included in the stored value).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - event_date (date)
   Comment: The date when the lineage was generated. This is a partitioned column.
   Definition: Event date for the lineage generation time; used for partitioning.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX
   Example Filters: 2025
 - record_id (string)
   Comment: Primary key of each row, it is auto-generated and cannot be joined with any tables
   Definition: Auto-generated primary key for the lineage row; intended as a unique row identifier and not joinable to other tables.
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - event_id (string)
   Comment: One query or one spark job run could append multiple lineage rows, this event_id is a unique id to group the rows that belong to the same event. This is generated in the pipeline and cannot be joined with any tables.
   Definition: Identifier to group multiple lineage rows produced by the same write event (single query or Spark job run); generated internally and not joinable to other tables.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - statement_id (string)
   Comment: A foreign key to join with query history system table. It is set when a query is from a warehouse or serverless warehouse.
   Definition: Foreign key to Databricks SQL query history for warehouse/serverless executions; populated when the event originates from a SQL statement context.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

TABLE: system.access.inbound_network
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Audit inbound network requests and the associated access-control decisions
- Investigate denied inbound requests to identify blocked sources or misconfigurations
- Monitor trends in inbound access decisions over time (allow vs deny)

TABLE: system.access.outbound_network
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Audit denied egress events by workspace and time window for security/compliance reporting
- Troubleshoot why a domain/IP/storage endpoint is unreachable from notebooks/jobs
- Identify most frequently blocked destinations and destination types to refine network policies
- Correlate denial patterns with specific access types and destination details (DNS vs storage)

- Troubleshoot why a domain/IP/storage endpoint is unreachable from notebooks/jobs

- Correlate denial patterns with specific access types and destination details (DNS vs storage)

TABLE: system.access.table_lineage
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Build end-to-end lineage graphs from upstream sources to downstream targets for governance and impact analysis
- Investigate who/what wrote to a table or path and from which inputs, at a specific time or run
- Join to query history/jobs/pipelines to attribute lineage to executions and operational metadata (status, duration, cluster/warehouse)
- Allocate costs/usage to downstream assets by connecting billing/usage to the producing queries/jobs via statement_id or run identifiers

TABLE: system.access.table_lineage
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Build end-to-end lineage graphs from upstream sources to downstream targets for governance and impact analysis

- Allocate costs/usage to downstream assets by connecting billing/usage to the producing queries/jobs via statement_id or run identifiers

TABLE: system.access.table_lineage
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.query.history
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.access.table_lineage.statement_id = system.query.history.statement_id, system.access.table_lineage.statement_id = system.query.history.cache_origin_statement_id, system.access.table_lineage.statement_id = system.query.history.cache_origin_statement_id AND system.access.table_lineage.statement_id = system.query.history.statement_id, system.access.table_lineage.source_table_full_name = system.access.table_lineage.source_table_full_name
 - system.lakeflow.jobs
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.access.table_lineage.entity_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id, system.access.table_lineage.entity_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id AND system.access.table_lineage.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.workspace_id, system.access.table_lineage.event_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id
 - system.lakeflow.pipelines
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.access.table_lineage.entity_id = system.lakeflow.pipelines.pipeline_id
 - system.access.workspaces_latest
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.table_lineage.workspace_id = system.access.workspaces_latest.workspace_id
 - system.access.audit
   Join Types: LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.access.audit.entity_id = system.access.table_lineage.entity_id, system.access.audit.event_id = system.access.table_lineage.event_id

TABLE: system.access.workspaces_latest
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Join to billing usage to attribute costs/consumption to workspace name and URL
- Filter or report on active vs non-active workspaces using lifecycle status
- Enrich audit logs with workspace metadata for governance and investigations
- Create an inventory of workspaces in the account with creation timestamps

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Compute run-time and wall-clock duration trends for jobs and runs over time
- Join to billing usage and compute/cluster metadata to attribute cost and resource usage to job runs
- Monitor failures and termination reasons (result_state, termination_code/type) for alerting and reliability reporting
- Analyze queue/setup/execution/cleanup phase durations to identify orchestration and performance bottlenecks

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.lakeflow.jobs
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id AND system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.workspace_id, system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id, system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id AND system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.last_run_end_time = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.last_run_end_time
 - system.billing.usage
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.billing.usage.job_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id AND system.billing.usage.run_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.run_id, system.billing.usage.usage_time = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.period_start_time, system.billing.usage.job_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id AND system.billing.usage.job_run_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.run_id AND system.billing.usage.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.workspace_id, system.billing.usage.job_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id AND system.billing.usage.run_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.run_id AND system.billing.usage.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.workspace_id
 - system.lakeflow.job_task_run_timeline
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.run_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.run_id, system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.run_id = system.lakeflow.job_task_run_timeline.parent_run_id, system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.run_id = system.lakeflow.job_task_run_timeline.job_run_id
 - system.compute.clusters
   Join Types: LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.compute.clusters.cluster_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.compute_ids, system.compute.clusters.cluster_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.cluster_id
 - system.compute.node_timeline
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.compute.node_timeline.cluster_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.cluster_id

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_task_run_timeline
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Compute task run durations and identify long-running tasks by slicing and summing timeline periods
- Analyze task outcomes (result_state/termination) over time and correlate failures with compute used
- Attribute task run activity to clusters/warehouses and join to compute/node timelines for performance investigation
- Build job/task run monitoring dashboards with time-windowed utilization and phase durations

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_task_run_timeline
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_tasks
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Audit task configuration changes over time (who/when via timestamps, and what changed via attributes like depends_on_keys, timeout_seconds, health_rules)
- Reconstruct historical job task DAGs (dependencies) for a point-in-time view
- Detect and report task deletions and the last-known configuration prior to deletion
- Monitor adoption of newer configuration fields such as timeout_seconds and health_rules after late Nov 2025

TABLE: system.lakeflow.job_tasks
DOC_TYPE: table_columns
Columns:
 - account_id (string)
 - workspace_id (string)
 - job_id (string)
 - task_key (string)
 - depends_on_keys (array<string>)
 - change_time (timestamp)
 - delete_time (timestamp)
 - timeout_seconds (bigint)
 - health_rules (array<struct<metric:string,operator:string,value:bigint>>)

TABLE: system.lakeflow.jobs
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Reconstruct historical job configuration as-of a timestamp and compare config changes over time
- Join to job run timeline to attribute run behavior/cost to the job configuration in effect at run time
- Inventory and governance reporting (who created jobs, who runs as, tags, deployment metadata)
- Operational monitoring for paused jobs, timeouts, schedules/triggers, and health rule settings

TABLE: system.lakeflow.jobs
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id AND system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.workspace_id, system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id, system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.job_id AND system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.last_run_end_time = system.lakeflow.job_run_timeline.last_run_end_time
 - system.lakeflow.pipelines
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.lakeflow.jobs.name = system.lakeflow.pipelines.name
 - system.access.workspaces_latest
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.access.workspaces_latest.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.workspace_id
 - system.access.table_lineage
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.access.table_lineage.entity_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.job_id AND system.access.table_lineage.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.jobs.workspace_id
 - system.access.audit
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.audit.user_email = system.lakeflow.jobs.creator_user_name

TABLE: system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Measure pipeline update durations and time spent per slice/hour across updates
- Track update outcomes (result_state) by trigger type and update type
- Identify retries/restarts of the same update via request_id and correlate to trigger details
- Attribute update execution to compute type/cluster and run-as identity for governance and troubleshooting

TABLE: system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.lakeflow.pipelines
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline.pipeline_id = system.lakeflow.pipelines.pipeline_id
   Extended Conditions: system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.pipelines.workspace_id
 - system.access.workspaces_latest
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.workspaces_latest.workspace_id = system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline.workspace_id

   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

TABLE: system.lakeflow.pipelines
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Audit pipeline configuration changes over time (who changed what and when).
- Reconstruct the pipeline settings/configuration that was active at the time of a run or update event.
- Track pipeline inventory and lifecycle events (created, modified, deleted) across workspaces/accounts.
- Segment/report pipelines by type, edition/channel, serverless/photon/continuous/development modes, and tags.

TABLE: system.lakeflow.pipelines
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.access.table_lineage
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.access.table_lineage.entity_id = system.lakeflow.pipelines.pipeline_id
 - system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.lakeflow.pipeline_update_timeline.pipeline_id = system.lakeflow.pipelines.pipeline_id
 - system.lakeflow.job_tasks
   Join Types: FULL_OUTER
   Join on: system.lakeflow.job_tasks.month = system.lakeflow.pipelines.month

TABLE: system.lakeflow.zerobus_stream
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Monitor stream activity volume and timelines by workspace/account
- Troubleshoot ingestion failures using recorded error details
- Analyze stream lifecycles (open-to-close durations) and identify abnormal closures
- Join to zerobus_ingest on stream_id to correlate stream events with ingest outcomes

TABLE: system.lakeflow.zerobus_stream
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.lakeflow.zerobus_ingest
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.lakeflow.zerobus_ingest.stream_id = system.lakeflow.zerobus_stream.stream_id

   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

   Comment: Data format used for passing data
   Definition: Payload/data encoding format used for data passed through the stream.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - errors (array<struct<error_code:bigint,error_message:string,timestamp:timestamp>>)
   Comment: The errors that occurred while using the stream
   Definition: Collection of error instances observed for the stream usage, including code, message, and timestamp per error.
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT

TABLE: system.mlflow.runs_latest
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- List and filter runs by experiment, status, time range, tags, or creator
- Join to experiments_latest to report experiment-to-run coverage and run outcomes
- Use aggregated_metrics for quick comparisons (min/max/latest) without reading metric history
- Identify deleted (soft-deleted) runs and last update times for governance/monitoring

TABLE: system.mlflow.runs_latest
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.mlflow.run_metrics_history
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.mlflow.run_metrics_history.run_id = system.mlflow.runs_latest.run_id
 - system.access.table_lineage
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.access.table_lineage.created_by = system.mlflow.runs_latest.created_by
 - system.mlflow.experiments_latest
   Join Types: INNER
   Join on: system.mlflow.experiments_latest.experiment_id = system.mlflow.runs_latest.experiment_id

TABLE: system.serving.endpoint_usage
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Attribute LLM token consumption (input/output) by served_entity_id, requester, and usage_context keys
- Track request volumes and error rates over time using request_time and status_code
- Estimate serving cost by joining to reference rate tables (for example cost rates) and aggregating usage measures
- Operational analytics: compare streaming vs non-streaming request patterns and their associated usage

TABLE: system.serving.endpoint_usage
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Attribute LLM token consumption (input/output) by served_entity_id, requester, and usage_context keys

- Estimate serving cost by joining to reference rate tables (for example cost rates) and aggregating usage measures
- Operational analytics: compare streaming vs non-streaming request patterns and their associated usage

TABLE: system.serving.endpoint_usage
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.serving.served_entities
   Join Types: ANTI, INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.serving.endpoint_usage.served_entity_id = system.serving.served_entities.served_entity_id
 - system.governance.cost_rates
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.governance.cost_rates.endpoint_name = system.serving.endpoint_usage.endpoint_name AND system.governance.cost_rates.served_entity_name = system.serving.endpoint_usage.served_entity_name

TABLE: system.serving.endpoint_usage
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.serving.served_entities
   Join Types: ANTI, INNER, LEFT
   Join on: system.serving.endpoint_usage.served_entity_id = system.serving.served_entities.served_entity_id
 - system.governance.cost_rates
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.governance.cost_rates.endpoint_name = system.serving.endpoint_usage.endpoint_name AND system.governance.cost_rates.served_entity_name = system.serving.endpoint_usage.served_entity_name

   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - requester (string)
   Comment: The ID of the user or service principal whose permissions are used for the invocation request of the serving endpoint.
   Definition: Identity (user or service principal) whose permissions were used to invoke the serving endpoint; represents the authenticated caller.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: NULL, alexander.booth@databricks.com, anlei.huang@databricks.com, azamat.omuraliev@databricks.com, george.theodosopoulos@databricks.com
 - status_code (int)
   Comment: The HTTP status code that was returned from the model.
   Definition: HTTP response status code returned for the invocation, indicating success or error category.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 200, 429, 500
 - request_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The timestamp at which the request is received.
   Definition: Timestamp when Databricks received the serving request.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
   Example Filters: 2025-01-01 00:00:00, 2025-05-15, 2025-09-26T00:00:00.000Z, 2025-10-15, 2025-11-25T00:00:00.000Z
 - input_token_count (bigint)
   Comment: The token count of the input.
   Definition: Number of tokens in the request input (for example prompt tokens) attributed to this invocation.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, SUM
   Example Filters: 0
 - output_token_count (bigint)
   Comment: The token count of the output.
   Definition: Number of tokens in the response output (for example completion tokens) attributed to this invocation.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
   Example Filters: 0
 - input_character_count (bigint)
   Comment: The character count of the input string or prompt.
   Definition: Character length of the input string/prompt for this request.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - output_character_count (bigint)
   Comment: The character count of the output string of the response.
   Definition: Character length of the response text produced for this request.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, SUM
 - usage_context (map<string,string>)
   Comment: The user provided map containing identifiers of the end user or the customer application that makes the call to the endpoint. See [Further define usage with usage_context](https://docs.databricks.com/en/ai-gateway/configure-ai-gateway-endpoints.html#usage-context).
   Definition: User-provided key/value attributes describing the end user or calling application (for example tenant, app, customer identifiers) to support downstream attribution and chargeback.
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - request_streaming (boolean)
   Comment: Whether the request is in stream mode.
   Definition: Indicates whether the invocation used streaming responses (stream mode) versus a single non-streaming response.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - served_entity_id (string)
   Comment: The unique ID used to join with the system.serving.served_entities dimension table to lookup information about the endpoint and served entity.
   Definition: Identifier of the served entity that handled the request; join key to system.serving.served_entities to retrieve endpoint and served-entity metadata.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: ad3799e1a94b3471a404feff6f5edd2c, d5908e5396a03bd89a7b206dd079ff8f

TABLE: system.serving.served_entities
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Inventory all served entities by workspace/endpoint and entity type (custom, foundation, external, feature spec).
- Audit configuration history over time (throughput/concurrency/compute) for a given endpoint or served entity.
- Attribute serving usage and costs by joining to endpoint_usage and billing usage via served_entity_id/endpoint_id.
- Identify when served entities were removed by endpoint deletion time for compliance and lifecycle management.

TABLE: system.serving.served_entities
DOC_TYPE: relationships
Relationships:
 - system.serving.endpoint_usage
   Join Types: LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.serving.endpoint_usage.served_entity_id = system.serving.served_entities.served_entity_id, system.default.serving_endpoints_raw.endpoint_name = system.serving.endpoint_usage.endpoint_name
 - system.billing.usage
   Join Types: INNER, LEFT
   Extended Conditions: system.billing.usage.endpoint_id = system.serving.served_entities.endpoint_id, system.billing.usage.endpoint_name = system.serving.served_entities.endpoint_name
 - system.governance.cost_rates
   Join Types: LEFT
   Join on: system.governance.cost_rates.endpoint_name = system.serving.endpoint_usage.endpoint_name AND system.governance.cost_rates.served_entity_name = system.serving.endpoint_usage.served_entity_name

   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - task (string)
   Comment: The task type. Can be llm/v1/chat, llm/v1/completions, or llm/v1/embeddings.
   Definition: Serving task/API mode used by the endpoint (llm/v1/chat, llm/v1/completions, llm/v1/embeddings).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - external_model_config (struct<provider:string>)
   Comment: Configurations for external models. For example, {Provider: OpenAI}
   Definition: Configuration payload specific to external model providers (for example, provider name such as OpenAI).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - foundation_model_config (struct<min_provisioned_throughput:bigint,max_provisioned_throughput:bigint>)
   Comment: Configurations for foundation models. For example,{min_provisioned_throughput: 2200, max_provisioned_throughput: 4400}
   Definition: Configuration payload for foundation models, including provisioned throughput limits (min/max).
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - custom_model_config (struct<min_concurrency:int,max_concurrency:int,compute_type:string>)
   Comment: Configurations for custom models. For example,{ min_concurrency: 0, max_concurrency: 4, compute_type: CPU }
   Definition: Configuration payload for custom models, including concurrency limits and compute type.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - feature_spec_config (struct<min_concurrency:int,max_concurrency:int,compute_type:string>)
   Comment: Configurations for feature specifications. For example, { min_concurrency: 0, max_concurrency: 4, compute_type: CPU }
   Definition: Configuration payload for feature specification serving, including concurrency limits and compute type.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - change_time (timestamp)
   Comment: Timestamp of change for the served entity.
   Definition: Timestamp when this served entity record/config version became effective (change event time).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - endpoint_delete_time (timestamp)
   Comment: Timestamp of entity deletion. The endpoint is the container for the served entity. After the endpoint is deleted, the served entity is also deleted.
   Definition: Timestamp when the serving endpoint was deleted; after this time the contained served entities are considered deleted.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: -, NULL

TABLE: system.storage.predictive_optimization_operations_history
DOC_TYPE: use_cases
Use Cases:
- Audit which tables were compacted or vacuumed, when, and whether the operations succeeded or failed
- Analyze operational duration and cadence of predictive optimization by workspace/metastore/catalog/schema/table
- Investigate failed optimization operations and correlate to specific tables or environments
- Support usage reporting and chargeback using usage_unit and usage_quantity

   Comment: ID of the account.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - workspace_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the workspace in which predictive optimization ran the operation.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - start_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time at which the operation started. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when the optimization operation began.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MIN
 - end_time (timestamp)
   Comment: The time at which the operation ended. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
   Definition: Timestamp when the optimization operation completed (successfully or not).
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT, MAX, MIN
 - metastore_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the metastore to which the optimized table belongs.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - metastore_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the metastore to which the optimized table belongs.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - catalog_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the catalog to which the optimized table belongs.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: sevann_demo
 - schema_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the schema to which the optimized table belongs.
   Cardinality: medium
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: poc_neosoft
 - table_name (string)
   Comment: The name of the optimized table.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
   Example Filters: silver_reseau_bt
 - table_id (string)
   Comment: The ID of the optimized table.
   Cardinality: high
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - operation_type (string)
   Comment: The optimization operation which was performed. The value will be COMPACTION or VACUUM.
   Definition: Type of predictive optimization action performed (e.g., COMPACTION or VACUUM).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - operation_id (string)
   Comment: The ID for the optimization operation.
   Definition: Unique identifier for a single predictive optimization operation execution.
   Cardinality: unique
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - operation_status (string)
   Comment: The status of the optimization operation. The value will be SUCCESSFUL or FAILED: INTERNAL_ERROR.
   Definition: Outcome status of the operation (e.g., SUCCESSFUL or FAILED with an internal error classification).
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - operation_metrics (map<string,string>)
   Comment: Additional details about the specific optimization that was performed.
   Definition: Key-value metrics describing what the operation did and/or its effects (for example, files processed, bytes rewritten, or other operation-specific details).
   Cardinality: unknown
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
 - usage_unit (string)
   Comment: The unit of usage that this operation incurred. 
   Definition: Unit used to measure billable/attributed usage incurred by the optimization operation.
   Cardinality: low
   Common Aggregations: AVG, COUNT
